title: change language_detection_threshold default value
body:
Summary:
- The current multilingual language-detection loop in the transcribe path breaks after the first language detection when language_detection_threshold is None due to this condition: if language_detection_threshold is None or language_probability > language_detection_threshold: break
- This effectively ignores language_detection_segments beyond the first segment, making language_detection_segments irrelevant when no explicit threshold is provided.
- Proposed change: modify the condition so that we only break early if a threshold is actually provided and the detected probability exceeds that threshold. This preserves language_detection_segments behavior even when language_detection_threshold is not explicitly set by the user.
- Implementations affected: both the non-batched (WhisperModel.transcribe) and batched (BatchedInferencePipeline.transcribe) code paths in faster_whisper/transcribe.py.
- Expected impact: language detection will respect language_detection_segments by scanning up to language_detection_segments segments when no threshold is given, improving robustness for languages that require more context for confident detection. No public API changes beyond the threshold semantics; default behavior remains effectively “no early-break” unless a threshold is supplied.
- Testing notes: add tests to verify that with language_detection_segments > 1 and language_detection_threshold=None, more than one segment can contribute to language selection; also verify that when language_detection_threshold is set, the early-break behavior remains as intended. Update existing tests if necessary.
- Risks: low risk to existing decoding accuracy; performance impact is minimal (a few extra language-detection passes when threshold is None, which is the default).

step_plan.summary: Modify language detection threshold semantics so that early-break only occurs when a real threshold is provided, allowing language_detection_segments to influence language selection even when language_detection_threshold is None. This applies to both non-batched (WhisperModel.transcribe) and batched (BatchedInferencePipeline.transcribe) paths.

steps.operation: Refactor threshold-based break condition in non-batched language detection loop
steps.reason: Currently the loop breaks after the first language detection when language_detection_threshold is None due to an unconditional break, which ignores language_detection_segments. The new condition should only break when a real threshold is provided, preserving multi-segment detection behavior when threshold is not set by the user.
steps.side_effects: Requires updates to unit/integration tests for language_detection_segments > 1 with language_detection_threshold=None; minor potential performance impact due to evaluating multiple segments; downstream components relying on threshold-based early exit remain unaffected when a threshold is provided.

steps.operation: Apply the same threshold-aware break refactor in the batched language detection path
steps.reason: Ensure consistent behavior across the batched transcription path by applying the same conditional change so early exit occurs only when language_detection_threshold is not None and the probability exceeds the threshold.
steps.side_effects: Tests for the batched path will need updates; potential minor performance implications similar to the non-batched path; API behavior remains unchanged when a threshold is supplied.
